title: coldenia plicata
intro: Caldenia plicata, also known as Mexican tea, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and distinctive leaflets. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Caldenia plicata include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism and arthritis, and treating urinary tract infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water or as a decoction, which is often used to treat various health issues.
